name: Microsoft
philosophy: Microsoft's web presence is institutional-scale and product-broad — the brand's design language Fluent 2 ladders from consumer Windows to enterprise Azure to Surface hardware to Office, and the site stitches them together with a near-monochrome chrome and a single chromatic accent — Microsoft Blue (#0067c0) — used scarcely on CTAs and on the four-square logo. The atmosphere is utilitarian and corporate without feeling cold; product imagery (Surface devices, screen renders of Windows, Teams collaboration shots) carries the warmth.

design_language.motion_signature: Fluent 2 motion: gentle 250ms cubic-bezier ease-in-out on transitions, subtle scale-up (1.02) on card hover, no spring overshoots. The acrylic blur (backdrop-filter) is the system's signature material — used on flyouts, command bars, and sticky navigation.

trademark_signals: Microsoft Blue (#0067c0) as the single chromatic accent — used on primary CTAs and the four-square logo. No secondary brand color exists in the marketing chrome
Segoe UI Variable — the proprietary system font — runs display through body in a continuous weight axis. Headlines lean into the heavier display optical sizes
Fluent 2 acrylic backdrop blur on flyouts, command bars, and sticky chrome — the brand's signature material
Four-square logo (red/green/blue/yellow) as the only multi-chromatic asset in the system — used only as the brand mark, never as decoration
Card-based product grids with 4–8px radius and a soft 1px hairline border — the Fluent surface unit
ai_slop_avoided: Don't replace Segoe UI with Inter or a geometric sans — the humanist proportions are core to the Fluent feel
Don't make the four-square logo decorative; it is a mark, not a pattern
Don't add a second brand accent color — Microsoft Blue is the only chromatic CTA color
Don't ignore the acrylic-blur material — flat translucent overlays read as off-brand
